evals.prompt: How should I organise a large React project's folder structure?
evals.expected_output: Use feature-based organisation from bulletproof-react: src/features/{name}/{components,hooks,api,types,index.ts}. Each feature exposes a public API through index.ts — never import from deep internal paths. Shared UI primitives go in src/components/, app-wide hooks in src/hooks/, third-party wrappers in src/lib/.

evals.prompt: My component has too many props. How do I avoid prop drilling?
evals.expected_output: First, co-locate state closer to where it's used — lift only when two components genuinely share it. For complex parent-child component APIs, use compound components: the parent provides context via React.createContext, child sub-components (Select.Option, etc.) consume it internally. For pure logic sharing, extract a custom hook.

evals.prompt: Should I use render props or custom hooks for logic sharing in React?
evals.expected_output: Custom hooks — render props are mostly superseded by hooks. Use render props only when you need to inject rendering into a lifecycle boundary (e.g. a virtualised list's renderItem). For all logic sharing (data fetching, form state, timers), extract a custom hook starting with 'use'.
